strengths: 18+ years of progressive engineering experience with appropriate seniority (current Staff Engineer); demonstrated director-level IC credentials
Proven technical leadership through workshops, open-source platforms (GISCollective, Trial, Fluent-Asserts), mentoring programs, and roles teaching advanced concepts
Entrepreneurial background (GISCollective founder since 2016, multiple startup experiences) with strong polyglot technical foundation across web, mobile, embedded, and infrastructure domains
concerns: AI expertise is recent and infrastructure-focused (MCP, agentic workflows at re:cinq); lacks demonstrated AI-powered products or platforms, which is central to the Principal Engineer AI Strategy role
Educational background is bachelor's-only (CS + Fine Arts degrees); missing the preferred Master's/PhD in AI-related field for a principal-level AI advisor role

Cloud platform expertise appears limited—mentions AWS CDK and microservices but no evidence of cloud architect/strategist experience at the depth expected for advising strategic customers on cutting-edge cloud/AI adoption
response.justification: Candidate has strong engineering fundamentals, appropriate seniority, and clear leadership capability, but the role requires 'recognized expertise in AI with demonstrated products or platforms built with AI' and the ability to advise on cutting-edge adoption. Candidate's AI experience is recent (current role, ~14 months) and infrastructure-focused rather than product-focused. While the trajectory is promising—recent move into AI-agent infrastructure shows interest—there is insufficient evidence of AI strategy depth or product expertise to confidently fill a Principal Engineer role centered on AI differentiation for a cloud/AI platform. Would warrant a conversation to assess AI knowledge depth and growth trajectory.
